title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory · Spiral Systems Review
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the versioned cross-language ownership ABI contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned cross-language ownership ABI after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
